title.zh: 动词分类：及物/不及物、动作/状态、谓语/非谓语

summary.en: The same verb can be sorted three ways: by whether it needs an object (transitive vs intransitive), by whether it moves (action vs state), and by whether it can be the one predicate verb of a clause (finite vs non-finite).
summary.zh: 同一个动词可以从三个角度分类：看是否需要宾语（及物 vs 不及物），看是否真的动起来（动作 vs 状态），以及看能否做句子的谓语动词（谓语/限定 vs 非谓语/非限定）。

heading.en: Transitive vs intransitive — does it need an object?
heading.zh: 及物 vs 不及物 —— 需要宾语吗？

body.en: A verb that needs no receiver of the action — no object — is intransitive (e.g. the verb in 'The rabbit slept.'). A verb that needs a receiver — an object — is transitive (e.g. the verbs in patterns 2 through 4). The Chinese term 及物 literally means 'reach / carry an object', i.e. the action has a target, and that target is the object.
body.zh: 无需动作承受者、也就是无需宾语的动词，是不及物动词（如 The rabbit slept. 里的动词）。需要动作承受者、也就是需要宾语的动词，是及物动词（如句型二到四里的动词）。“及物”的“及”是达到、关联、带着的意思，所以“及物”就是动作有对象，而这个对象就是动词的宾语。

concepts.sourceRef: 无需承受者，也就是宾语的动词

heading.zh: 动作动词 vs 状态动词

body.en: Transitive and intransitive verbs together are the lexical (full) verbs, also called action/behavior verbs, because they carry real meaning. Sorted instead by motion, lexical verbs split into action verbs (e.g. eat — clearly something moving) and state verbs (e.g. have, feel, own, believe — relatively still, nothing really 'moves').
body.zh: 及物动词和不及物动词合起来就是实意动词，也叫行为动词，因为它们有实际意义。如果改按动作与状态来分，实意动词又分为动作动词（如 eat 吃，明显动起来了）和状态动词（如 have 有、feel 感觉、own 拥有、believe 相信，相对静止，没有真正动起来）。

concepts.sourceRef: 如果按照状态动作来分，实意动词还可分为动作动词和状态动词

body.en: A linking verb assigns a state or quality: before it is the subject, after it is the subject complement (also called the predicative). Traditional grammar lists it as its own class because its meaning is weak — it mostly just links subject and complement. Some systems instead file it under intransitive verbs, and a linking verb can usually be seen as a state verb.
body.zh: 系动词（联系动词）用来赋予状态或性质：它前面是主语，后面是主语补语，又称表语。传统语法常把它单列一类，因为它实际意义不强，往往只是联系主语和表语。但也有些语法体系把系动词算作不及物动词，而系动词往往也可被视作状态动词。

concepts.sourceRef: 用来赋予状态性质的系动词

heading.zh: 谓语（限定）动词 vs 非谓语（非限定）动词

body.en: Lexical verbs and linking verbs can serve as the predicate verb; auxiliaries and modals cannot stand alone as the verb. A simple sentence has exactly ONE predicate verb, which can express tense-aspect-mood; any other verb in it must appear as a non-finite (non-predicate) verb, which cannot carry tense-aspect-mood.
body.zh: 实意动词和系动词可以做句子的谓语动词；助动词和情态动词不能独立作动词。一个简单句只能有一个谓语动词，它可以表达时态、语态、语气；句中其他动词必须以非谓语动词（非限定动词）的身份出现，无法表达时态语气。

concepts.sourceRef: 一个简单句只能有一个谓语动词

heading.en: Complete vs incomplete transitive (a note on terminology)
heading.zh: 完全及物 vs 不完全及物（术语小注）

body.en: You may also hear 'complete transitive' vs 'incomplete transitive' — that simply means whether an object complement is needed (pattern 2 vs pattern 4). As a learner, don't argue over exactly which box a verb belongs in; grammar systems differ and even shift over time. What matters is understanding the usage behind the term.
body.zh: 你也可能听到“完全及物”和“不完全及物”这样的术语，其实就是在说是否需要宾语补语（句型二 vs 句型四）。作为学习者，不必纠结某个词究竟该归到哪一类——语法体系有很多种，同一体系也会随时间改变。最重要的是理解术语背后的用法。

concepts.sourceRef: 完全及物动词和不完全及物动词这样的术语，其实就是在说是否需要宾语补语

note.en: No object is needed — 'slept' is intransitive.
note.zh: 不需要宾语——“slept”是不及物动词。

note.en: 'Ate' needs a receiver — the object 'a carrot'. One object, no complement = (complete) transitive.
note.zh: “ate”需要承受者——宾语“a carrot”。一个宾语、无需补语 = （完全）及物。

examples.text: The rabbit gave the wolf a carrot.

note.en: An action verb taking two objects: indirect 'the wolf' + direct 'a carrot'.
note.zh: 带两个宾语的动作动词：间接宾语“the wolf”+ 直接宾语“a carrot”。

examples.text: The rabbit considered the carrot tasty.

note.en: One object plus an object complement 'tasty' = incomplete transitive. 'Considered' is also a state verb (believing).
note.zh: 一个宾语再加宾语补语“tasty” = 不完全及物。“considered”也属状态动词（认为/相信）。

note.en: 'Is' links the subject to the subject complement 'smart' (the predicative). A linking verb, often viewed as a state verb.
note.zh: “is”把主语和主语补语（表语）“smart”联系起来。系动词，常被视作状态动词。

note.en: 'Owns' (have/possess) is a transitive verb that is also a state verb — nothing physically moves. Here it is the one finite predicate verb of the clause.
note.zh: “owns”（拥有）是及物动词，同时也是状态动词——没有真正动起来。它在此句中是唯一的谓语（限定）动词。

prompt.en: In 'The rabbit slept.', what kind of verb is 'slept'?
prompt.zh: 在“The rabbit slept.”中，“slept”是哪类动词？

text.zh: 不及物动词——不需要宾语

text.en: Linking verb — it needs a subject complement

explain.en: Sleeping needs no receiver of the action, so 'slept' is intransitive.
explain.zh: “睡”不需要动作承受者，所以“slept”是不及物动词。

prompt.en: Which verb is a STATE verb rather than an action verb?
prompt.zh: 下列哪个是状态动词，而不是动作动词？

explain.en: 'Believe' (like have, feel, own) is relatively still — a state verb. 'Eat' and 'give' clearly involve motion.
explain.zh: “believe”（和 have、feel、own 一样）相对静止——状态动词。“eat”和“give”明显有动作。

prompt.en: A simple sentence can have only ONE ____ verb; every other verb must appear as a non-finite verb. (one word)
prompt.zh: 一个简单句只能有一个____动词；其余动词都必须以非谓语动词出现。（填一个词）

explain.en: Only the predicate (finite) verb carries tense-aspect-mood; the rest must be non-finite.
explain.zh: 只有谓语（限定）动词承载时态语气；其余必须是非谓语动词。

explain.en: 'Slept' is intransitive and cannot take the object 'a carrot'. Use a transitive verb like 'ate' to take an object.
explain.zh: “slept”是不及物动词，不能带宾语“a carrot”。要带宾语得用及物动词，如“ate”。

title.zh: 把动词从三个角度归类

lines.speaker: Rabbit
lines.text: And then the rabbit ate a carrot.

note.en: One object — transitive, and an action verb.
note.zh: 一个宾语——及物，且是动作动词。

lines.speaker: Wolf
lines.text: The rabbit considered the carrot tasty.

note.en: Object + object complement — incomplete transitive; also a state verb.
note.zh: 宾语 + 宾语补语——不完全及物；也是状态动词。

lines.speaker: Rabbit
lines.text: Either way, the rabbit is smart.

note.zh: 系动词 + 主语补语（表语）。
